requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Rule 3 of OI Rules, the Reserve Bank is empowered to administer these Rules
and to issue regulations and directions/ circulars as it may deem necessary, for the effective
implementation of the provisions of these Rules.
requirements.object: effective implementation of the provisions of these Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
AD Category-I Banks may bring
the contents of these Directions to the notice of their customers / constituents concerned.
requirements.object: these Directions to the notice of their customers / constituents concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
Yours faithfully
(N Senthil Kumar) Chief General Manager
Master Direction - Overseas Investment
These Directions shall be called Master Direction - Overseas Investment which shall be read with the provisions contained in Foreign Exchange Management (Overseas Investment) Rules, 2022 (hereinafter referred to as "OI Rules") and Foreign Exchange Management (Overseas Investment) Regulations, 2022 (hereinafter referred to as "OI Regulations").
requirements.object: - Overseas Investment which shall be read with the provisions contained in Foreign Exchange Management (Overseas Investment

requirements.full_sentence:
Reference shall be made to OI Rules and OI Regulations for the definitions of the various
terms used in these directions.
requirements.object: Rules and OI Regulations for the definitions of the various terms used in these directions

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the major terms are reiterated and elaborated below:
Explanation: Once an investment in a foreign entity is classified as ODI, the investment
shall continue to be treated as ODI even if such investment falls below 10% of the paid-up
equity capital or the investor loses control in the foreign entity.
requirements.object: as ODI even if such investment falls below 10% of the paid-up equity capital or the investor loses control in the foreign entit

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'investment fund overseas, duly regulated' for the purpose of this para shall
also include funds whose activities are regulated by financial sector regulator of host
country or jurisdiction through a fund manager.
requirements.object: activities are regulated by financial sector regulator of host country or jurisdiction through a fund manager

requirements.full_sentence:
f) Resident individuals may make OPI within the overall limit for Liberalised Remittance
Scheme (LRS) in terms of schedule III of the OI Rules.
requirements.object: overall limit for Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) in terms of schedule III of the OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, shares or interest acquired
by the resident individuals by way of sweat equity shares or minimum qualification shares
or under Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP)/ Employee Benefits Scheme up to 10%
of the paid up capital/stock, whether listed or unlisted, of the foreign entity and without
control shall also qualify as OPI.

g) Any investment made overseas in accordance with schedule IV of the OI Rules in
securities as stipulated by SEBI by Mutual Funds (MFs), Venture Capital Funds (VCFs)
and Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) registered with SEBI shall be considered as OPI.

(i) "foreign entity" - the extant concept of Joint Venture (JV) and Wholly Owned
Subsidiary (WOS) is substituted under the new regime with the concept of foreign entity, which
means an entity formed or registered or incorporated outside India, including in International
Financial Services Centre (IFSC) in India, that has limited liability.
requirements.object: registered or incorporated outside India, including in International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) in India, that has limited l

conditions:
In case of a foreign entity being an
investment fund or vehicle
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of a foreign entity being an
investment fund or vehicle, duly regulated by the regulator for the financial sector in the host
jurisdiction and set up as a trust outside India, the liability of the person resident in India shall
be clear and limited not exceeding the interest or contribution in the fund in any manner.
requirements.object: not exceeding the interest or contribution in the fund in any manner

requirements.full_sentence: Further, the trustee of such fund shall be a person resident outside India.
requirements.object: outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) "strategic sector" shall include energy and natural resources sectors such as Oil,
Gas, Coal, Mineral Ores, submarine cable system and start-ups and any other sector or sub-
sector as deemed fit by the Central Government.
requirements.object: resources sectors such as Oil, Gas, Coal, Mineral Ores, submarine cable system and start-ups and any other sector or sub- s

requirements.full_sentence:
The restriction of limited liability structure of
foreign entity shall not be mandatory for entities with core activity in any strategic sector.
requirements.object: with core activity in any strategic sector

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks may allow remittances for ODI in strategic sector after ensuring that Indian
entity has obtained necessary permission from the competent authority, wherever applicable.
requirements.object: in strategic sector after ensuring that Indian entity has obtained necessary permission from the competent authority

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) "control" means the right to appoint majority of the directors or to control the
management or policy decisions exercisable by a person or persons acting individually or in
concert, directly or indirectly, including by virtue of their shareholding or management rights or
shareholders' agreements or voting agreements that entitle them to ten percent or more of
voting rights or in any other manner in the entity.
requirements.object: majority of the directors or to control the management or policy decisions exercisable by a person or persons acting individually

conditions:
where all the investors from
India in a foreign entity were together considered as IP
requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) "Indian entity" - the extant concept of Indian party (IP) where all the investors from
India in a foreign entity were together considered as IP, has been substituted under the new
regime with the concept of Indian entity where each investor entity shall be separately
considered as an Indian entity.
requirements.object: an Indian entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Indian entity shall mean a company defined under the
Companies Act, 2013 or a body corporate incorporated by any law for the time being in force
or a Limited Liability Partnership formed under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008 or a
partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932.
requirements.object: under the Companies Act, 2013 or a body corporate incorporated by any law for the time being in force or a Limited Liability Pa

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) "subsidiary"/ "step down subsidiary (SDS)" of a foreign entity means an entity in
which the foreign entity has control and the structure of such subsidiary/SDS shall comply with
the structural requirements of a foreign entity, i.e., such subsidiary/SDS shall also have limited
liability where the foreign entity's core activity is not in strategic sector.
requirements.object: requirements of a foreign entity, i

requirements.full_sentence:
The investee entities
of the foreign entity where such foreign entity does not have control (as defined above) shall
not be treated as SDSs and therefore need not be reported henceforth.
requirements.object: and therefore need not be reported henceforth

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) "Overseas Direct Investment (ODI)" means (i) acquisition of any unlisted equity
capital or subscription as a part of the Memorandum of Association of a foreign entity, or (ii)
investment in 10% or more of the paid-up equity capital of a listed foreign entity, or (iii)
investment with control where investment is less than 10% of the paid-up equity capital of a
listed foreign entity.
requirements.object: unlisted equity capital or subscription as a part of the Memorandum of Association of a foreign entity, or (ii) investment in 1

requirements.full_sentence:
Exemptions from applicability of OI Rules/Regulations/Directions
The provisions contained in the OI Rules/Regulations/Directions shall not apply, and general
permission shall be available for acquisition or transfer of any investment outside India made
as per rule 4 of the OI Rules.
requirements.object: shall be available for acquisition or transfer of any investment outside India made as per rule 4 of the OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
Permission for making overseas investment
(1) A person resident in India may make or transfer any investment or financial
commitment outside India under general permission/automatic route subject to the provisions
contained in the OI Rules, OI Regulations and these directions.
requirements.object: investment or financial commitment outside India under general permission/automatic route subject to the provisions contained in

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, overseas
investment may be made in a foreign entity engaged in a bona fide business activity, directly
or through SDS/special purpose vehicle (SPV).
requirements.object: foreign entity engaged in a bona fide business activity, directly or through SDS/special purpose vehicle (SPV)

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The person intending to make any financial commitment shall fill up the Form FC as
provided in the "Master Direction - Reporting under Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999" duly supported by the requisite documents and approach the designated AD bank for
making the investment/remittance.
requirements.object: FC as provided in the "Master Direction - Reporting under Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999" duly supported by the requisite docu

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 2In respect of any case under the approval route, the applicant shall approach their
designated AD bank who shall forward the proposal to the concerned Regional Office of the
Reserve Bank after due scrutiny and with its specific recommendations.
requirements.object: bank who shall forward the proposal to the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank after due scrutiny and with its

requirements.full_sentence:
The designated AD bank before forwarding the proposal
shall submit the relevant sections of the Form FC in the online OID application and the
transaction number generated by the application shall be mentioned in their reference.
requirements.object: of the Form FC in the online OID application and the transaction number generated by the application shall be mentioned i

requirements.full_sentence:
The
following documents shall be submitted along with the proposal:
• Background and brief details of the transaction.
requirements.object: the proposal: • Background and brief details of the transaction

requirements.full_sentence: o Benefits which may accrue to India through such investment;
requirements.object: such investment

requirements.full_sentence:
Approval from the Central Government
The applications for overseas investment/financial commitment in Pakistan/other jurisdiction
as may be advised by the Central Government from time to time or in strategic sectors/specific
geographies in accordance with rule 9 of OI Rules shall be forwarded by the AD banks from
their constituents to the Reserve Bank as per the laid down procedure for onward submission
to the Central Government.
requirements.object: AD banks from their constituents to the Reserve Bank as per the laid down procedure for onward submission to the Central Governmen

requirements.full_sentence:
Approval from the Reserve Bank
Financial commitment by an Indian entity, exceeding USD 1 (one) billion (or its equivalent) in
a financial year shall require prior approval of the Reserve Bank even when the total financial
commitment of the Indian entity is within the eligible limit under the automatic route.
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank even when the total financial commitment of the Indian entity is within the eligible limit under the automa

requirements.full_sentence:
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the lender bank/regulatory body/investigative
agency
(1) Any person resident in India having an account appearing as a Non-Performing Asset
(NPA) or is classified as wilful defaulter or is under investigation by a financial sector regulator/
investigative agency shall obtain an NOC from the lender bank/regulatory body/investigative
agency concerned in accordance with rule 10 of OI Rules, before making financial commitment
or undertaking disinvestment.
requirements.object: the lender bank/regulatory body/investigative agency concerned in accordance with rule 10 of OI Rules, before making financial comm

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where an Indian entity has already issued a guarantee in accordance with the FEMA
provisions before an investigation has begun or account is classified as NPA/wilful defaulter
and subsequently is required to honour such contractual obligation, such remittance due to
the invocation will not constitute fresh financial commitment and hence NOC shall not be
required.

Rights issue and bonus shares
(1) A person resident in India, who has acquired and continues to hold equity capital in a
foreign entity in accordance with the OI Rules/Regulations may acquire equity capital through
exercise of rights or by way of bonus shares in accordance with rule 7 of the OI Rules.
requirements.object: exercise of rights or by way of bonus shares in accordance with rule 7 of the OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The acquisition of equity capital through exercise of such rights shall be reported in
Form FC.
requirements.object: FC

requirements.full_sentence:
Where such person does not exercise the rights but renounces such rights in favour
of a person resident in India or a person resident outside India, such renouncement shall not
require reporting.

Further, the acquisition of bonus shares shall not be treated as fresh financial
commitment and will not require reporting.
requirements.object: financial commitment and will not require reporting

requirements.full_sentence:
Acquisition of a foreign entity through bidding or tender procedure
(1) AD banks may, on being approached by an eligible person resident in India, allow
remittance towards Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) after obtaining Form A2 duly filled in or may
issue bid bond guarantee on their behalf in accordance with regulation 9(5) of OI Regulations
for participation in bidding or tender procedure for acquisition of a foreign entity.
requirements.object: by an eligible person resident in India, allow remittance towards Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) after obtaining Form A2 duly fille

requirements.full_sentence:
On winning
the bid, AD banks may facilitate further remittances to the foreign entity so acquired after
obtaining Form FC duly filled in and report such financial commitment.
requirements.object: the foreign entity so acquired after obtaining Form FC duly filled in and report such financial commitment

conditions: in case they are not successful in the bid
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) AD banks, while permitting remittance towards EMD should advise the Indian
entity/investor that in case they are not successful in the bid, they shall repatriate the amount
remitted in accordance with Notification No.
requirements.object: in accordance with Notification No

conditions: where such a person resident in India
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In cases where such a person resident in India, after being successful in the bid/tender
decides not to proceed further with the investment, AD banks shall ensure the bona fides of
transaction while permitting the invocation of bid-bond guarantee or forfeiture of EMD.
requirements.object: of transaction while permitting the invocation of bid-bond guarantee or forfeiture of EMD

requirements.full_sentence:
ODI in startups
Any ODI in startups in accordance with rule 19(2) of OI Rules shall not be made out of funds
borrowed from others.
requirements.object: funds borrowed from others

requirements.full_sentence:
The AD bank, before facilitating the transaction, shall obtain necessary
certificate in this regard from the statutory auditors/chartered accountant of the Indian
entity/investor.
requirements.object: this regard from the statutory auditors/chartered accountant of the Indian entity/investor

requirements.full_sentence:
Acquisition or transfer by way of deferred payment
(1) AD bank shall verify the bona fides of the transactions from the underlying
agreement/documents in case of deferment of payment of consideration in accordance with
regulation 7 of OI Regulations.
requirements.object: of the transactions from the underlying agreement/documents in case of deferment of payment of consideration in accordance with

requirements.full_sentence: The period of deferment shall be defined upfront.

conditions:
In case the
remittance towards acquisition of equity capital is to be made post subscription to
Memorandum of Association
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the
remittance towards acquisition of equity capital is to be made post subscription to
Memorandum of Association, the period within which such remittance is to be made shall be
defined in the underlying agreement/documents/applicable laws else the remittance shall be
made on or before acquisition of/setting up of the foreign entity.
requirements.object: underlying agreement/documents/applicable laws else the remittance shall be made on or before acquisition of/setting up of the forei

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The part of the payment towards consideration deferred by the person resident in India
shall be treated as non-fund based financial commitment by such person and shall be reported
accordingly.
requirements.object: based financial commitment by such person and shall be reported accordingly

requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequent payments towards deferred consideration shall be reported in Form
FC as conversion of non-fund based financial commitment to equity.
requirements.object: FC as conversion of non-fund based financial commitment to equity

requirements.full_sentence:
The valuation in
accordance with pricing guidelines, wherever applicable, shall be done upfront.

Mode of Payment
The mode of payment by a person resident in India for making overseas investment shall be
in accordance with regulation 8 of the OI Regulations.
requirements.object: regulation 8 of the OI Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, no
remittance shall be made by any Indian entity to its branch/office outside India for making
any overseas investment.
requirements.object: Indian entity to its branch/office outside India for making any overseas investment

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) A person resident in India shall not make any payment on behalf of any foreign entity
other than by way of financial commitment as permitted under the OI Rules/Regulations.
requirements.object: behalf of any foreign entity other than by way of financial commitment as permitted under the OI Rules/Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Any investment/financial commitment in Nepal and Bhutan shall be done in a manner as
provided in Notification No.
requirements.object: manner as provided in Notification No

requirements.full_sentence:
All dues receivable
on investments (or financial commitment) made in freely convertible currencies, as well
as their sale/winding up proceeds are required to be repatriated to India in freely
convertible currencies only.
requirements.object: India in freely convertible currencies only

requirements.full_sentence:
Pricing Guidelines
(1) The AD bank, before facilitating an overseas investment related transaction, shall
ensure compliance with the provisions contained in rule 16 of OI Rules.
requirements.object: provisions contained in rule 16 of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
With respect to the
documents to be taken by the AD bank, they shall be guided by their board approved policy,
which may, inter alia, provide for taking into consideration the valuation as per any
internationally accepted pricing methodology for valuation.
requirements.object: board approved policy, which may, inter alia, provide for taking into consideration the valuation as per any internationally accept

requirements.full_sentence:
The AD bank shall put in place a
board approved policy within two months from the date of these directions3.
requirements.object: board approved policy within two months from the date of these directions3

requirements.full_sentence: 3 Directions means FEM(OI) Directions, 2022 issued vide A.P.
requirements.object: vide A

conditions:
where the valuation may not be insisted
upon
requirements.full_sentence:
12 dated August 22, 2022
(2) Such policy may also provide for scenarios where the valuation may not be insisted
upon, such as (i) transfer on account of merger, amalgamation or demerger or liquidation,
where the price has been approved by the competent Court/Tribunal as per the laws in India
and/or the host jurisdiction or (ii) price is readily available on a recognised stock exchange,
etc.
requirements.object: where the valuation may not be insisted upon, such as (i) transfer on account of merger, amalgamation or demerger or liquidation

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also clearly provide for additional documents such as the audited financial
statements of the foreign entity, etc.
requirements.object: additional documents such as the audited financial statements of the foreign entity, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
that may be taken by the AD banks for ascertaining the
bona fides in cases involving write-off of the investment.
requirements.object: AD banks for ascertaining the bona fides in cases involving write-off of the investment

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer or liquidation
A person resident in India holding equity capital in accordance with OI Rules may transfer such
investment in accordance with rule 17 of OI Rules.
requirements.object: accordance with rule 17 of OI Rules

conditions:
where the transferor is
required to repatriate all the dues before disinvestment
requirements.full_sentence:
It is clarified that where the transferor is
required to repatriate all the dues before disinvestment, such requirement shall not apply to
the dues that do not arise on account of investment in equity or debt like export receivables,
etc.
requirements.object: that do not arise on account of investment in equity or debt like export receivables, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Restructuring
(1) A person resident in India who has made ODI in a foreign entity, may permit
restructuring of the balance sheet by such foreign entity in accordance with rule 18 of OI Rules.
requirements.object: balance sheet by such foreign entity in accordance with rule 18 of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate investment in both the equity and debt of the foreign entity shall be taken into
consideration for computing the proportionate amount of accumulated losses.
requirements.object: for computing the proportionate amount of accumulated losses

requirements.full_sentence:
However, in
case the restructuring involves only equity, investment only in equity of the foreign entity may
be taken into consideration for computing proportionate losses.
requirements.object: for computing proportionate losses

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The certificate required to be furnished in accordance with rule 18 of OI Rules shall
mention the amount of accumulated losses as per the audited balance sheet of the foreign
entity, the proportionate amount of accumulated losses based upon the share of the Indian
entity/investor, the amount of diminution in the value of the outstanding dues towards the
Indian entity/investor post restructuring and that such diminution does not exceed the
proportionate amount of accumulated losses.
requirements.object: accumulated losses as per the audited balance sheet of the foreign entity, the proportionate amount of accumulated losses based

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) These provisions shall not be used where the assets are simply revalued in the books
of the Indian entity without any restructuring of the balance sheet of the foreign entity.
requirements.object: assets are simply revalued in the books of the Indian entity without any restructuring of the balance sheet of the foreign entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Opening of Foreign Currency Account abroad by an Indian entity
An Indian entity may open, hold and maintain Foreign Currency Account (FCA) abroad for the
purpose of making ODI in accordance with the provisions contained in Regulation 5 (D) of
Notification No.
requirements.object: Foreign Currency Account (FCA) abroad for the purpose of making ODI in accordance with the provisions contained in Regulation

requirements.full_sentence:
Obligations of the Person Resident in India
(1) A person resident in India making ODI shall meet the obligations laid down in regulation
9 of OI Regulations.
requirements.object: down in regulation 9 of OI Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A person resident in India acquiring equity capital in a foreign entity, which is reckoned
as ODI, shall submit the evidence of investment as per regulation 9(1) of OI Regulations to the
AD bank within six months, failing which the funds remitted overseas shall be repatriated within
the said period of six months.
requirements.object: investment as per regulation 9(1) of OI Regulations to the AD bank within six months, failing which the funds remitted overseas

requirements.full_sentence:
The evidence of investment shall be retained by the designated
AD bank, who shall monitor the receipt of required documents and satisfy themselves about
the bona fides of the documents so received.
requirements.object: designated AD bank, who shall monitor the receipt of required documents and satisfy themselves about the bona fides of the document

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Form FC shall be submitted along with requisite documents to AD bank for obtaining
UIN on or before making initial ODI.
requirements.object: requisite documents to AD bank for obtaining UIN on or before making initial ODI

requirements.full_sentence:
The AD bank after due verification shall report the details
in the OID application for allotment of UIN.
requirements.object: the OID application for allotment of UIN

requirements.full_sentence:
Any remittance towards a foreign entity shall be
facilitated by the AD bank only after obtaining the necessary UIN for such entity.
requirements.object: AD bank only after obtaining the necessary UIN for such entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Reporting
(1) All reporting with respect to overseas investment by a person resident in India shall be
made in accordance with regulation 10 of OI Regulations through the designated AD bank as
per the revised reporting forms and instructions contained in the "Master Direction - Reporting
under Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999".
requirements.object: with regulation 10 of OI Regulations through the designated AD bank as per the revised reporting forms and instructions containe

requirements.full_sentence:
Any incomplete filing shall be treated as non-
submission.
requirements.object: submission

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Any acquisition of foreign securities through conversion of Indian Depository Receipts
(IDRs) shall be duly reported as ODI or OPI, as applicable.
requirements.object: ODI or OPI, as applicable

conditions: where the statutory audit is not applicable
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The Annual Performance Report (APR) shall be certified by a chartered accountant
where the statutory audit is not applicable, including in case of resident individuals.
requirements.object: chartered accountant where the statutory audit is not applicable, including in case of resident individuals

conditions: where APR is required to be filed jointly
requirements.full_sentence:
It is also
clarified that where APR is required to be filed jointly, either one investor may be authorised
by other investors for filing APR, or such persons may jointly file the APR.
requirements.object: either one investor may be authorised by other investors for filing APR, or such persons may jointly file the APR

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A resident individual making overseas investment must comply with the reporting
requirements as provided under the OI Regulations and reporting shall also be done as
provided under the LRS where such investment is reckoned towards the LRS limit.
requirements.object: provided under the LRS where such investment is reckoned towards the LRS limit

requirements.full_sentence:
Acquisition
of foreign securities by way of inheritance or gift in accordance with paragraph 2 of Schedule
III of OI Rules shall not be reckoned towards the LRS limit and hence, shall not require
reporting under LRS.
requirements.object: LRS limit and hence, shall not require reporting under LRS

conditions:
In case a person resident in India has made a delay in filing/submitting the requisite
form/return/document
requirements.full_sentence:
Delay in Reporting
(1) In case a person resident in India has made a delay in filing/submitting the requisite
form/return/document, such person may file/submit the requisite form/return/ document, etc.
requirements.object: document, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The LSF for delay in reporting of overseas investment related transactions shall be
calculated as per the following matrix:
Sr.
requirements.object: the following matrix: Sr

conditions:
Where an advice has been issued for payment of LSF and such LSF is not paid within
30 days
requirements.full_sentence:
e) Where an advice has been issued for payment of LSF and such LSF is not paid within
30 days, such advice shall be considered as null and void and any LSF received
beyond this period shall not be accepted.

conditions:
If the applicant subsequently approaches for
payment of LSF for the same delayed reporting
requirements.full_sentence:
If the applicant subsequently approaches for
payment of LSF for the same delayed reporting, the date of receipt of such application
shall be treated as the reference date for the purpose of calculation of LSF.
requirements.object: reference date for the purpose of calculation of LSF

requirements.full_sentence:
f) The option of LSF shall be available up to three years from the due date of
reporting/submission under OI Regulations.
requirements.object: three years from the due date of reporting/submission under OI Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
The option of LSF shall also be available
for delayed reporting/submissions under the Notification No.
requirements.object: delayed reporting/submissions under the Notification No

requirements.full_sentence:
as per OI Regulations/earlier corresponding
regulations, neither makes such submission/filing within the specified time nor makes
such submission/filing along with LSF as provided in regulation 11 of OI Regulations,
such person shall be liable for penal action under the provisions of FEMA, 1999.
requirements.object: action under the provisions of FEMA, 1999

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The LSF may be paid by way of a demand draft drawn in favour of "Reserve Bank of
India" and payable at the Regional Office concerned (in accordance with UIN mapping given
in the table below).
requirements.object: of a demand draft drawn in favour of "Reserve Bank of India" and payable at the Regional Office concerned (in accordance with UIN mappi

requirements.full_sentence:
Restriction on further financial commitment or transfer
AD bank shall not facilitate any outward remittance/further financial commitment by a person
resident in India towards a foreign entity until any delay in reporting is regularised and may be
guided by regulation 12 of OI Regulations.
requirements.object: financial commitment by a person resident in India towards a foreign entity until any delay in reporting is

requirements.full_sentence:
Restrictions and prohibitions
(1) AD bank shall not facilitate any transaction in respect of any foreign entity engaged in
an activity mentioned in rule 19(1) of OI Rules or located in countries/ jurisdictions as advised
by the Central Government under rule 9(2) of OI Rules.
requirements.object: respect of any foreign entity engaged in an activity mentioned in rule 19(1) of OI Rules or located in countries/ jurisd

requirements.full_sentence:
It is clarified that financial products
linked to Indian Rupee shall include non-deliverable trades involving foreign currency-INR
exchange rates, stock indices linked to Indian market, etc.
requirements.object: foreign currency-INR exchange rates, stock indices linked to Indian market, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
It is provided that
no further layer of subsidiary or subsidiaries shall be added to any structure existing with two
or more layers of subsidiaries post notification of the OI Rules/Regulations.
requirements.object: structure existing with two or more layers of subsidiaries post notification of the OI Rules/Regulations

requirements.full_sentence: Note: It may be noted that subsidiary shall have the meaning as provided in the OI Rules i.e.
requirements.object: provided in the OI Rules i

requirements.full_sentence:
Financial commitment by an Indian entity
An Indian entity, within the overall limit provided in schedule I of the OI Rules and subject to
regulation 3 of OI Regulations, may make financial commitment by way of ODI as per schedule
I of the OI Rules, financial commitment by way of debt as per regulation 4 of OI Regulations
and non-fund based financial commitment as per regulations 5, 6 and 7 of OI Regulations.
requirements.object: way of ODI as per schedule I of the OI Rules, financial commitment by way of debt as per regulation 4 of OI Regulations a

conditions:
In case of swap of securities both the legs of transaction shall comply with FEMA
provisions
requirements.full_sentence:
The
following is further provided:
(1) In case of swap of securities both the legs of transaction shall comply with FEMA
provisions, as applicable.
requirements.object: as applicable

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Financial commitment by way of debt [regulation 4 of OI Regulations]- AD bank shall
facilitate an outward remittance towards financial commitment by way of debt only after
obtaining the necessary agreement/documents for ensuring the bona fides of the transaction.
requirements.object: towards financial commitment by way of debt only after obtaining the necessary agreement/documents for ensuring the b

requirements.full_sentence: An Indian entity shall not lend directly to its overseas SDS.
requirements.object: overseas SDS

requirements.full_sentence:
Further a resident individual shall
not make financial commitment by way of debt.
requirements.object: way of debt

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) It is provided with respect to financial commitment by way of Guarantee [regulation 5
of OI Regulations] that-
a) In the case of performance guarantee, time specified for the completion of the contract
shall be treated as its validity period.
requirements.object: validity period

requirements.full_sentence:
b) No prior approval from the Reserve Bank shall be needed for remitting the funds from
India on account of invocation of a performance guarantee extended in accordance with
OI Rules/Regulations.
requirements.object: the funds from India on account of invocation of a performance guarantee extended in accordance with OI Rules/Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
c) Any guarantee, to the extent of the amount invoked, shall cease to be a part of the non-
fund based financial commitment but will be considered as financial commitment by
way of debt.
requirements.object: part of the non- fund based financial commitment but will be considered as financial commitment by way of debt

requirements.full_sentence: Such invocation shall be reported in Form FC.
requirements.object: FC

requirements.full_sentence: d) Roll-over of guarantee shall not be treated as fresh financial commitment.
requirements.object: financial commitment

requirements.full_sentence:
However,
such roll-over shall be reported in Form FC.
requirements.object: FC

requirements.full_sentence:
e) A group company of the Indian entity may extend a guarantee in accordance with the
OI Regulations if such group company is eligible to make ODI as per the OI Rules and
such guarantee shall be counted towards the utilisation of the financial commitment limit
of such group company and shall be reported by the group company concerned.
requirements.object: utilisation of the financial commitment limit of such group company and shall be reported by the group company concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
In case
of a resident individual promoter, the same shall be counted towards the financial
commitment limit of the Indian entity and accordingly be reported by the Indian entity.
requirements.object: financial commitment limit of the Indian entity and accordingly be reported by the Indian entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, for computing the financial
commitment limit of the group company, any fund-based exposure of such group
company to the Indian entity or of the Indian entity to such group company, as the case
may be, shall be deducted from the net worth of such group company.
requirements.object: net worth of such group company

requirements.full_sentence:
Nil
(6) Financial commitment by way of pledge/charge shall be subject to following conditions:
a) The value of the pledge/charge or the amount of the facility, whichever is less, shall
be reckoned towards the financial commitment limit provided such facility has not
already been reckoned towards the limit prescribed;
requirements.object: conditions: a) The value of the pledge/charge or the amount of the facility, whichever is less, shall be reckoned towards the
or jurisdiction in which financial commitment is not permissible under the OI Rules
the relevant provisions of the Act or rules or regulations made, or directions issued thereunder

conditions: if not specified upfront
requirements.full_sentence:
e) The period of charge, if not specified upfront, shall be co-terminus with the period of
facility (like loan or other facility) for which charge has been created;
requirements.object: period of facility (like loan or other facility) for which charge has been created

requirements.full_sentence:
f) in the event of enforcement of charge created on domestic assets, such domestic
assets shall be transferred by way of sale to a person resident in India only;
requirements.object: of sale to a person resident in India only

requirements.full_sentence:
g) Wherever creation of charge involves pledge of shares of an Indian company in
favour of an overseas lender, the pledge shall also be governed by the extant FEMA
provisions contained in FEM (Non-Debt Instruments) Rules, 2019.
requirements.object: the extant FEMA provisions contained in FEM (Non-Debt Instruments) Rules, 2019

conditions: Where such investment is in IFSC
requirements.timeline:
within 45 days from the date of receipt of application complete in all respects failing which it shall be deemed to be approved
Not engaged in Financial Services activity
Subject to the guidelines issued by the respective regulator
b
requirements.full_sentence:
Where such investment is in IFSC, the requisite approval by the financial services regulator concerned shall be decided within 45 days from the date of receipt of application complete in all respects failing which it shall be deemed to be approved
Not engaged in Financial Services activity
Subject to the guidelines issued by the respective regulator
b) Not engaged in Financial Services activity
Engaged in Financial Services activity except banking or insurance
Indian entity has posted net profits during the preceding three financial years.
requirements.object: days from the date of receipt of application complete in all respects failing which it shall be deemed to be approved Not engaged

requirements.full_sentence: However, an Indian entity not meeting 3-year profitability condition may make such ODI in a foreign entity in IFSC in India.
requirements.object: a foreign entity in IFSC in India

requirements.full_sentence: c) Overseas investment in any sector by banks and non-banking financial institutions regulated by the Reserve Bank shall be subject to such other conditions as may be stipulated by the regulatory department concerned of the Reserve Bank in this regard.
requirements.object: other conditions as may be stipulated by the regulatory department concerned of the Reserve Bank in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
(8) Limit of financial commitment shall be governed by paragraph 3 of schedule I of the OI
rules.
requirements.object: 3 of schedule I of the OI rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The following is further provided:
a) Utilisation of the balances held in EEFC account, and the amount raised by issue of
American Depository Receipts (ADR)/ Global Depositary Receipts (GDR) and ADR/ GDR
stock-swap for making financial commitment shall also be reckoned towards the financial
commitment limit.
requirements.object: the financial commitment limit

requirements.full_sentence:
However, financial commitment made through such resources prior to the
date of notification of the OI Rules/Regulations shall not be reckoned towards the limit.
requirements.object: limit

conditions:
Where the proceeds from External Commercial Borrowings (ECB) are utilised for
making financial commitment
requirements.full_sentence:
b) Where the proceeds from External Commercial Borrowings (ECB) are utilised for
making financial commitment, the same shall be reckoned towards the financial commitment
limit.
requirements.object: financial commitment limit

requirements.full_sentence:
However, only that part of the ECB shall be reckoned towards the financial commitment
limit that exceeds the amount of the corresponding pledge or creation of charge on assets
which has already been counted towards the financial commitment limit.
requirements.object: financial commitment limit that exceeds the amount of the corresponding pledge or creation of charge on assets which has alrea

requirements.full_sentence:
A resident individual
may make overseas investment in accordance with schedule III of OI Rules.
requirements.object: accordance with schedule III of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The following is
further provided:
(1) Where a resident individual has made ODI without control in a foreign entity that
subsequently acquires or sets-up a subsidiary/SDS, such resident individual shall not acquire
control in such foreign entity.
requirements.object: foreign entity

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Overseas investment by way of capitalisation, swap of securities, rights/bonus, gift,
and inheritance shall be categorised as ODI or OPI based on the nature of the investment.
requirements.object: or OPI based on the nature of the investment

conditions: where the investment
requirements.full_sentence:
However, where the investment, whether listed or unlisted, by way of sweat equity shares,
minimum qualification shares and shares/interest under Employee Stock Ownership Plan
(ESOP)/Employee Benefits Scheme does not exceed 10 per cent of the paid-up capital/stock
of the foreign entity and does not lead to control, such Investment shall be categorised as OPI.

conditions:
In case of swap of securities both the legs of the transaction shall comply with FEMA
provisions
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In case of swap of securities both the legs of the transaction shall comply with FEMA
provisions, as applicable.
requirements.object: as applicable

conditions:
where swap of securities results in acquisition of any
equity capital which is not in conformity with the OI Rules/Regulations
requirements.full_sentence:
However, where swap of securities results in acquisition of any
equity capital which is not in conformity with the OI Rules/Regulations, e.g., ODI in foreign
entity engaged in financial services activity, foreign entity having a subsidiary/SDS, etc., such
equity capital must be disinvested within a period of six months from the date of such
acquisition.
requirements.object: period of six months from the date of such acquisition

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Shares/interest under ESOP/Employee Benefits Scheme - AD banks may allow
remittances, towards acquisition of the shares/interest in an overseas entity under the scheme
offered directly by the issuing entity or indirectly through a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV)
/SDS.
requirements.object: of the shares/interest in an overseas entity under the scheme offered directly by the issuing entity or indirec

conditions: Where the investment qualifies as OPI
requirements.full_sentence:
Where the investment qualifies as OPI, the necessary reporting in Form OPI shall be
done by the employer concerned in accordance with regulation 10(3) of OI Regulations.
requirements.object: employer concerned in accordance with regulation 10(3) of OI Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
Where
such investment qualifies as ODI, the resident individual concerned shall report the transaction
in Form FC.
requirements.object: Form FC

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Though there is no limit on the amount of remittance made towards acquisition of
shares/interest under ESOP/Employee Benefits Scheme or acquisition of sweat equity shares,
such remittances shall be reckoned towards the LRS limit of the person concerned.
requirements.object: LRS limit of the person concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
Overseas investment by a person resident in India, other than an Indian entity or a
resident individual
A person resident in India, other than an Indian entity or a resident individual may make
overseas investment in accordance with schedule IV of OI Rules.
requirements.object: accordance with schedule IV of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The following is further
provided:
(1) Mutual Funds (MFs) and Venture Capital Funds (VCFs)/Alternative Investment Funds
(AIFs) registered with SEBI may, in accordance with paragraph 2 of schedule IV of OI Rules,
invest overseas in securities as stipulated by SEBI within an overall cap of USD 7 billion and
USD 1.5 billion, respectively.
requirements.object: paragraph 2 of schedule IV of OI Rules, invest overseas in securities as stipulated by SEBI within an overall cap of USD 7 billio

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, a limited number of qualified MFs are permitted to invest
cumulatively up to USD 1 billion in overseas Exchange Traded Funds, as may be permitted
by SEBI.

Such investment shall be considered as OPI irrespective of whether the securities
are listed or not.
requirements.object: irrespective of whether the securities are listed or not

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) MFs/VCFs/AIFs desirous of availing this facility may approach SEBI for necessary
permission.
requirements.object: permission

requirements.full_sentence:
Operational modalities regarding eligibility criteria, individual limits, identification
of recognised stock exchanges, investible universe, monitoring of aggregate ceilings, etc.,
shall be as per the guidelines issued by SEBI.
requirements.object: guidelines issued by SEBI

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An AD bank, including its overseas branch, may acquire or transfer foreign securities
in terms of host country regulations/laws, as applicable, in the normal course of its banking
business.
requirements.object: securities in terms of host country regulations/laws, as applicable, in the normal course of its banking business

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions contained in OI Rules/Regulations shall not apply to such acquisition
or transfer of foreign securities by an AD bank.
requirements.object: or transfer of foreign securities by an AD bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank in India, being licensed by the Reserve Bank under the provisions of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, may acquire the shares of Society for Worldwide Interbank
Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) as per the by-laws of SWIFT, provided the bank has
been permitted by the Reserve Bank for admission to the 'SWIFT User's Group in India' as a
member.
requirements.object: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) as per the by-laws of SWIFT

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Any overseas investment by the sole proprietorship or unregistered partnership firms
may be made by the proprietor concerned or the individual partners concerned within their
limit available under the LRS in accordance with schedule III of the OI Rules.
requirements.object: proprietor concerned or the individual partners concerned within their limit available under the LRS in accordance with schedule III of

conditions:
If the proposed
investment is in strategic sector
requirements.full_sentence:
If the proposed
investment is in strategic sector, any application for making overseas investment in excess of
the LRS limit may be made under the government approval route.
requirements.object: government approval route

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Overseas investment by registered trust/society may be made under the approval route
in accordance with paragraph 1 of schedule IV of OI Rules.
requirements.object: approval route in accordance with paragraph 1 of schedule IV of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
Overseas investment in an IFSC in India by a person resident in India
A person resident in India may make overseas investment in an IFSC in India in accordance
with schedule V of OI Rules.
requirements.object: an IFSC in India in accordance with schedule V of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The following is further provided:
(1) 4A person resident in India, being an Indian entity or a resident individual, may make
investment (including sponsor contribution) in units or any other instrument (by whatever name
called) issued by an investment fund or vehicle set up in an IFSC, as OPI.
requirements.object: contribution) in units or any other instrument (by whatever name called) issued by an investment fund or vehicle se

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, in
addition to listed Indian companies and resident individuals, unlisted Indian entities also may
make such investment in IFSC.
requirements.object: IFSC

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The restriction of making ODI only in an operating foreign entity or not making ODI in
a foreign entity engaged in financial services activity by resident individuals, shall not apply to
an investment made in IFSC.
requirements.object: made in IFSC

requirements.full_sentence:
Such investment, however, shall not be made in any foreign
entity engaged in banking or insurance.
requirements.object: foreign entity engaged in banking or insurance

requirements.full_sentence:
Such foreign entity in IFSC may have subsidiary/SDS
in IFSC.

It may also have subsidiary/SDS outside IFSC where the resident individual does not
have control in the foreign entity.
requirements.object: IFSC where the resident individual does not have control in the foreign entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Resident individual who has made ODI without control shall
not acquire control in a foreign entity that subsequently acquires or sets-up a subsidiary/SDS
outside India.
requirements.object: foreign entity that subsequently acquires or sets-up a subsidiary/SDS outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
Acquisition or Transfer of Immovable Property outside India
Any acquisition or transfer of immovable property outside India shall be governed by the
provisions contained in rule 21 of OI Rules.
requirements.object: provisions contained in rule 21 of OI Rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The following is further provided:
(1) An AD bank may allow an Indian entity having an overseas office to acquire immovable
property outside India for the business and residential purposes of its staff, provided total
remittances do not exceed the following limits as laid down for initial and recurring expenses,
respectively:
a) 15 per cent of the average annual sales/income or turnover of the Indian entity during the
last two financial years or up to 25 per cent of the net worth, whichever is higher;
requirements.object: having an overseas office to acquire immovable property outside India for the business and residential purposes of its staff

requirements.full_sentence:
Designated banks
(1) An eligible person resident in India making ODI (or financial commitment) in a foreign
entity is required to route all its transactions relating to such investment (or financial
commitment) through the AD bank designated by it.
requirements.object: transactions relating to such investment (or financial commitment) through the AD bank designated by it

requirements.full_sentence:
09 dated June 7, 2024
two or more persons resident in India, then all such persons shall route all transactions in
respect of that foreign entity only through one designated AD bank.
requirements.object: respect of that foreign entity only through one designated AD bank

conditions: if required
requirements.full_sentence:
However, different AD
banks may be designated, if required, for different foreign entities.

conditions: In case such a person resident in India wants to switch over to another AD
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In case such a person resident in India wants to switch over to another AD, it may
approach the new AD after obtaining an NOC from the existing AD bank.
requirements.object: after obtaining an NOC from the existing AD bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 5All communication from the person resident in India to the concerned Regional Office
of the Reserve Bank should be routed through the nodal branch of the designated AD bank.
requirements.object: nodal branch of the designated AD bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Such communication shall be made by the AD bank through the relevant form available on the
PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in/pravaah), in accordance with UIN mapping given
in the table below.
requirements.object: AD bank through the relevant form available on the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah

requirements.full_sentence:
KO or MA RO Chennai
For proper follow up, the AD bank shall maintain person-wise record in respect of each foreign
entity.
requirements.object: respect of each foreign entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Overseas investment under OI Rules/Regulations
(1) AD banks may allow remittance towards overseas investment up to the permissible
limits on receipt of application in Form FC together with form A-2, duly filled in, from the person
making such investments subject to their complying with the conditions prescribed in the OI
Rules/Regulations/Directions.
requirements.object: investment up to the permissible limits on receipt of application in Form FC together with form A-2, duly filled in,

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks shall render themselves liable for penal action under
section 11 and 13 of FEMA, 1999, if they facilitate remittances towards financial commitment
without obtaining the requisite duly completed Form FC.
requirements.object: penal action under section 11 and 13 of FEMA, 1999

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: AD banks may note that an additional timeline of 15 days is made
available to them for reporting of investments/financial commitment by their constituents to
RBI in the OID application (other than first remittance, which requires to be reported in OID
5 Para amended vide A.P.
requirements.object: timeline of 15 days is made available to them for reporting of investments/financial commitment by their constituents to RBI i

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) AD banks should allow remittance towards loan to the foreign entity and/or issue bank
guarantee to/on behalf of the foreign entity only after ensuring that the Indian entity has made
ODI and has control in the foreign entity.
requirements.object: to the foreign entity and/or issue bank guarantee to/on behalf of the foreign entity only after ensuring that the Indian

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The Reserve Bank will not, generally, specify the documents which should be verified
by the AD banks for ensuring the bona fides of the transactions.
requirements.object: AD banks for ensuring the bona fides of the transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
In this connection, attention
of authorised persons is drawn to sub-section (5) of Section 10 of the FEMA, 1999 which
provides that an authorised person shall require any person desiring to transact in foreign
exchange to make such a declaration and to give such information as will reasonably satisfy
him that the transaction will not involve and is not designed for the purpose of any
contravention or evasion of the provisions of the FEMA, 1999 or any rule, regulation,
notification, direction or order issued thereunder.
requirements.object: to transact in foreign exchange to make such a declaration and to give such information as will reasonably satisfy him tha

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks shall put in place a standard policy
laying down the requirements or documents or information to be obtained by their branches to
ensure compliance with said provisions of FEMA.
requirements.object: standard policy laying down the requirements or documents or information to be obtained by their branches to ensure compliance with sai

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) AD banks shall ensure bona fides of the transaction, compliance with FEMA provisions,
compliance with Know Your Customer (KYC)6 Guidelines and compliance with anti-money
laundering guidelines/laws.
requirements.object: the transaction, compliance with FEMA provisions, compliance with Know Your Customer (KYC)6 Guidelines and compliance with anti-m

requirements.full_sentence:
Any doubtful case/suspicious transaction shall be referred to
Directorate of Enforcement (DoE) for further investigation and necessary action.
requirements.object: of Enforcement (DoE) for further investigation and necessary action

conditions: In case of ODI by way of capitalisation of export proceeds or other entitlements
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of ODI by way of capitalisation of export proceeds or other entitlements, Indian
entity/resident individual shall make an application in Form FC to its designated AD bank.
requirements.object: Form FC to its designated AD bank

requirements.full_sentence:
AD
bank may ensure necessary reporting in Form FC, compliance with OI Rules/Regulations and
necessary reporting in EDPMS, wherever applicable, for facilitating such transactions.
requirements.object: Form FC, compliance with OI Rules/Regulations and necessary reporting in EDPMS

conditions: where such proceeds are overdue beyond the period specified for realisation/repatriation
requirements.full_sentence:
In case
where such proceeds are overdue beyond the period specified for realisation/repatriation,
before permitting such capitalisation AD bank may grant necessary extension post proper due
diligence.
requirements.object: proper due diligence

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Pre-incorporation expenses - AD bank may allow remittance towards pre-
incorporation expenses after satisfying itself of the reasonableness, up to a maximum of USD
100,000 per foreign entity.
requirements.object: incorporation expenses after satisfying itself of the reasonableness, up to a maximum of USD 100,000 per foreign entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Such remittances made by a resident individual shall be reckoned
towards their LRS limit.
requirements.object: LRS limit

requirements.full_sentence:
A person resident in India may capitalise pre-incorporation expenses
(i.e., financial commitment by way of ODI) or recognise such expenses as receivables (i.e.,
financial commitment by way of debt) or account them as expenses in their books.

that unless recognised as financial commitment such expenses shall not attract provisions of
OI Rules/Regulations.
requirements.object: Rules/Regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
The AD Maker shall initiate the transaction and submit to the AD Checker for verification before
submission to Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: submit to the AD Checker for verification before submission to Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
In case of delay in reporting for any reason the transaction shall
be reported to RBI only after a middle management level officer designated as an authoriser
ratifies it by recording the reasons for the same.
requirements.object: only after a middle management level officer designated as an authoriser ratifies it by recording the reasons for the same

conditions: if not obtained already
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The AD Maker, AD Checker and AD Authoriser as above identified by the AD Bank
may obtain a user-id, if not obtained already, for accessing the online OID application by
submitting a request in the prescribed format as per appendix A.
(3) The online reporting shall be made by the Centralised Unit/Nodal Office of AD banks.
requirements.object: Centralised Unit/Nodal Office of AD banks

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks shall be responsible for the validity of the information reported
online.
requirements.object: validity of the information reported online

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) AD banks should put in place proper processes and systems and issue necessary
instructions to all the dealing officials at the bank/branch level to ensure compliance with these
directions.
requirements.object: processes and systems and issue necessary instructions to all the dealing officials at the bank/branch level to ensure compliance
